heading: Foreign mail as United States mail
label: 1692 Foreign mail as United States mail
text: Every foreign mail, while being transported across the territory of the United States under authority of law, is mail of the United States, and any depredation thereon, or offense in respect thereto, shall be punishable as though it were United States mail.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 359 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 22935 Stat. 1134Minor changes were made in phraseology and obvious surplusage omitted.
